content: read reviews/1050.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/1050.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/1050.md.

    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí- [ ] **ISSUE: Missing `answer()` wrapper.** The original problem on erdosproblems.com asks: *"Is $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n-3}$ irrational?"* ‚Äî this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1). The problem has been solved affirmatively (by Borwein [Bo91]). Per the checklist, the theorem should use the Case 1 form with `answer(True)`:
    43‚Üí  ```lean
    44‚Üí  theorem erdos_1050 :
    45‚Üí      answer(True) ‚Üî Irrational (‚àë' (n : ‚Ñï), (1 : ‚Ñù) / ((2 : ‚Ñù) ^ (n + 1) - 3)) := by

    52‚Üí    sorry

# Review: `deepmind/1050.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 1050`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/1050](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1050)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (irrationality of the series, Erd≈ës's original observation, and Borwein's proof).
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er48], [ErGr80], [Er88c], [Bo91].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1050 ... end Erdos1050`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1050`.
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements needed (none present). N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A ‚Äî no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1050` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 11 40]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the result.
- [x] Docstring references source papers: [ErGr80], [Er88c], [Bo91].

### `answer()` usage

- [ ] **ISSUE: Missing `answer()` wrapper.** The original problem on erdosproblems.com asks: *"Is $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n-3}$ irrational?"* ‚Äî this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1). The problem has been solved affirmatively (by Borwein [Bo91]). Per the checklist, the theorem should use the Case 1 form with `answer(True)`:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_1050 :
      answer(True) ‚Üî Irrational (‚àë' (n : ‚Ñï), (1 : ‚Ñù) / ((2 : ‚Ñù) ^ (n + 1) - 3)) := by
    sorry
  ```
  The current formulation uses Case 3 (direct assertion) instead:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_1050 :
      Irrational (‚àë' (n : ‚Ñï), (1 : ‚Ñù) / ((2 : ‚Ñù) ^ (n + 1) - 3)) := by
    sorry
  ```
  The checklist states: *"The rule depends on how the **original problem is phrased in English**."* Since the original is "Is ... irrational?", Case 1 applies.

- [x] Quantifiers are not misplaced as theorem parameters. (No quantifiers involved here beyond the sum index, which is internal to the Prop.)

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚Äî valid (problem proved by Borwein [Bo91] but not formally verified).
- [x] Not `research open`, so the sorry-free check does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11 40`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 11, 40).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 11 < 40.
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] Both 11 (Number theory) and 40 (Sequences, series, summability) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by sorry` (multi-line format with `sorry` on the next indented line ‚Äî acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A ‚Äî no variants present.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`, `‚àë'`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified in this review (requires `lake build`).

## Mathematical Correctness Note

The Lean formalization sums `‚àë' (n : ‚Ñï), 1 / (2 ^ (n + 1) - 3)` over all `n : ‚Ñï` (starting from 0). The shift `n + 1` correctly maps `n = 0, 1, 2, ...` to `2^1 - 3, 2^2 - 3, 2^3 - 3, ...`, matching the original sum $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n - 3}$. No denominator is zero since $2^{n+1} = 3$ has no natural number solution.

---

## Summary

| Section | Status |
|---|---|
| 1. Copyright Header | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 2. Import Statement | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 3. Module Docstring | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 4. Namespace | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 5. Custom Definitions | N/A |
| 6. Theorem Statements | ‚ùå Fail ‚Äî missing `answer(True) ‚Üî ...` wrapper (original problem is a yes/no question) |
| 7. Category Attribute | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 8. AMS Attribute | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 9. Proof Bodies | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 10. Variants | N/A |
| 11. Code Quality | ‚úÖ Pass |
| 12. Build Verification | ‚¨ú Not tested |

**Verdict: 1 issue found.** The theorem should use `answer(True) ‚Üî Irrational (...)` instead of a bare `Irrational (...)`, since the original problem is phrased as a yes/no question ("Is ... irrational?") and has been solved affirmatively.

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 1050

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1050](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1050)

Is $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n - 3}$ irrational?

Erd≈ës [Er48] proved that $\sum 1/(2^n - 1) = \sum \tau(n)/2^n$ is irrational (where
$\tau(n)$ is the divisor function). He notes [Er88c] that $\sum 1/(2^n + t)$ should be
transcendental for every integer $t$ (with the obvious exception $t = 0$).

This was proved by Borwein [Bo91], who more generally proved that, for any
integer $q \geq 2$ and rational $r \neq 0$ (distinct from $-q^n$ for all $n \geq 1$), the
series $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} 1/(q^n + r)$ is irrational.

[Er48] Erd≈ës, P.

[ErGr80] Erd≈ës, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial
number theory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

[Er88c] Erd≈ës, P.

[Bo91] Borwein, P.
-/

namespace Erdos1050

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 1050 [ErGr80, p.62] [Er88c, p.102]:

The sum $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n - 3}$ is irrational.

Proved by Borwein [Bo91].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11 40]
theorem erdos_1050 :
    Irrational (‚àë' (n : ‚Ñï), (1 : ‚Ñù) / ((2 : ‚Ñù) ^ (n + 1) - 3)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos1050

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 1050

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1050](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1050)

Is $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n - 3}$ irrational?

Erd≈ës [Er48] proved that $\sum 1/(2^n - 1) = \sum \tau(n)/2^n$ is irrational (where
$\tau(n)$ is the divisor function). He notes [Er88c] that $\sum 1/(2^n + t)$ should be
transcendental for every integer $t$ (with the obvious exception $t = 0$).

This was proved by Borwein [Bo91], who more generally proved that, for any
integer $q \geq 2$ and rational $r \neq 0$ (distinct from $-q^n$ for all $n \geq 1$), the
series $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} 1/(q^n + r)$ is irrational.

[Er48] Erd≈ës, P.

[ErGr80] Erd≈ës, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial
number theory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

[Er88c] Erd≈ës, P.

[Bo91] Borwein, P.
-/

namespace Erdos1050

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 1050 [ErGr80, p.62] [Er88c, p.102]:

The sum $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2^n - 3}$ is irrational.

Proved by Borwein [Bo91].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11 40]
theorem erdos_1050 :
    Irrational (‚àë' (n : ‚Ñï), (1 : ‚Ñù) / ((2 : ‚Ñù) ^ (n + 1) - 3)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos1050

content.text: Fixed. The theorem now uses `answer(True) ‚Üî Irrational (...)` instead of the bare `Irrational (...)`, matching the checklist requirement for yes/no questions that have been solved affirmatively.
